counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If KL Rahul Hadn't Edged Off the Last Ball of Over 16?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: KL Rahul survives Over 16.6 (doesn't nick the wide delivery from Siraj)
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
KL Rahul had turned this chase from a mountain into a molehill. Ninety-two from fifty-two was the innings DC needed — dominant, precise, relentless against every bowler except the one that finally got him. Siraj's wide yorker in the final ball of over 16 was the exact delivery GT had workshopped. Rahul bit. One edge, one catch, one game changed.

Had he held his shape, left that wide one as he'd left three others in the same over, Rahul would have faced the last three overs alongside Miller — the two best-equipped DC batsmen for exactly this situation. With Rahul striking at 176 and needing 45 from 24 balls, that equation becomes comfortable. Even at his death-overs baseline of 140 SR, Rahul adds 11 runs across 8 balls. DC reach 220.

Prasidh's final over becomes academic. The last-ball drama never happens. Miller never has to take on the slower bouncer alone. One wide delivery, one decision, and the entire architecture of the final three overs collapses.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Miller Had Called Kuldeep Through on Ball 19.5?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: David Miller runs the single off 19.5, bringing DC to 210/8 needing 1 from 1
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: GT won by 1 run — Miller out run out (Buttler) last ball
counterfactual_scenarios.counterfactual_outcome: DC had ~60% chance of winning needing 1 from 1 ball
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
He sent Kuldeep back. In real time, it looked like a calculation: Miller on strike, Prasidh to face, one ball, two runs. More winnable, surely, than one run off a delivery where Kuldeep might be runout again. But the math didn't hold.

With 2 off 1, Prasidh had every option. He chose a slower bouncer — the exact delivery that neutralises Miller's power game. It hit him high, he missed, and Buttler's underarm throw did the rest. The win probability at that moment was barely 18%.

With 1 off 1, the equation looks different. Prasidh still has options, but DC need only an inside edge, a wide, a no-ball, a leg-bye — anything. The probability of scoring exactly 1 run from 1 ball, at 60%+, is three times more likely than scoring exactly 2. Miller's instinct to stay on strike — to back himself — was cricket's great gamble. But Kuldeep, running a single, might have been the most valuable delivery of the match.

game_changers.name: KL Rahul
game_changers.key_stat: 92 (52b, 11x4, 4x6) SR 176.92 — highest score in the match
game_changers.reason: Rahul's innings was the architecture of DC's chase. He absorbed Rashid Khan's threat, accelerated through the middle, and took DC from 101/3 at the halfway point to 166/6 by over 16. His dismissal — an edge off a wide Siraj yorker — cut the win probability from ~65% to ~35% in a single delivery.

game_changers.name: Prasidh Krishna
game_changers.key_stat: 4-0-42-2, ER 10.50 — last-over specialist, 13 needed from his final over
game_changers.reason: Prasidh was entrusted with the final over when GT needed five wickets and DC needed thirteen. He conceded a four (Nigam), took a wicket (Nigam caught Gill), got a run-out assist, conceded a six to Miller, then — with two needed off one — bowled the slower bouncer that Miller couldn't reach. Three overs earlier, nobody predicted this script.

game_changers.name: Rashid Khan
game_changers.reason: When DC were 76/1 and threatening a canter, Rashid delivered the most economical spell in the match. He took Rana and Rizvi off back-to-back deliveries in over 9 and later dismissed Axar, shifting the match landscape from a DC run-chase on auto-pilot to a genuine contest.

game_changers.name: Shubman Gill
game_changers.reason: Gill's 70 was the spine of GT's 210. He saw off the powerplay without getting himself out, then accelerated through the middle overs — the partnership with Buttler (60 runs, 31 balls) was the match's most productive batting unit per ball until the Rahul-Nissanka stand. Every time DC appeared to stabilise their bowling, Gill found an extra gear.

turning_points.title: The Double Blow
turning_points.description: Back-to-back wickets from Rashid Khan. Rana caught at long-off for 5, then Rizvi bowled through the gate second ball. DC went from 101/1 and cruising to 101/3 in the space of two deliveries. The required rate, manageable at 8.5 from 63 balls, suddenly had no batsman capable of anchoring it.

turning_points.title: The KL Dismissal
turning_points.description: KL Rahul had just scored 6, 4, 6 off Siraj in over 16 to drag DC back into contention. With DC needing 45 from 22 balls, Siraj returned to bowl the last ball — a full delivery wide of off. Rahul chased it and edged behind. In that single delivery, the match turned from DC's to GT's.

turning_points.title: The Miller Six
turning_points.description: With DC needing 8 from 3 balls and Prasidh bowling, Miller launched a length delivery over long-off for six. The ball was found, a new one requested. Two needed from two. The crowd held its breath. Miller had nearly delivered the impossible.
